rationale: In the introduction and Table 1, the cited study is not just listed; it is summarized for its specific finding that WOM-acquired customers stay longer and generate more referrals. The citing paper uses this finding to frame the broader acquisition-mode literature and justify why post-acquisition retention and value should differ by how customers were acquired. Although the new paper does not reuse Villanueva et al.'s VAR approach, the citation plays a clear conceptual and positioning role in motivating the research question.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Villanueva et al. (2008) as prior empirical evidence that acquisition mode (WOM vs marketing-induced) affects downstream customer lifetime and referral outcomes. This helps establish the premise that acquisition conditions have lasting behavioral effects and motivates the citing paper's free-trial retention and CLV study.
